# CLAUDE.md — HaleHubIOS

Native iOS client for HaleHub (SwiftUI, Swift 6, iOS 17+). Backend API at
`https://flyhomemn.com/api`. Sibling backend repo: `../halehub`.

## ⚠️ This project is generated by XcodeGen — `project.yml` is the source of truth

`HaleHubIOS.xcodeproj` is **regenerated** by `xcodegen generate`. Anything added
through Xcode's UI (targets, capabilities/entitlements, shared schemes, build
settings) is **silently wiped** on the next regeneration.

**Rule: never configure the project in Xcode's UI. Edit `project.yml`, then run
`xcodegen generate`.** This has bitten us before — the Safari Share Extension,
the App Group entitlement, and the shared scheme were all added via the UI, got
dropped on regen, and shipped broken to TestFlight (build 61 had no extension).

Everything reproducible now lives in `project.yml`:
- The `ShareExtension` app-extension target, embedded into the app.
- `CODE_SIGN_ENTITLEMENTS` for both targets (App Group `group.com.halefamily.halehubios`).
- The `HaleHubIOS` shared scheme.
- Versions: `MARKETING_VERSION` / `CURRENT_PROJECT_VERSION` in `settings.base`
  (bump with `scripts/bump_build.sh <n>`); the extension inherits them.

**Before archiving for TestFlight**, run `./scripts/verify_project.sh` — it
regenerates and asserts the extension, entitlements, and scheme survived. If it
fails, do not archive. See `docs/SHARE_EXTENSION.md`.

## Working across both repos
- When adding a backend API field, update the matching Swift model the same session.
- Always pull AND push BOTH repos (`halehub` + `HaleHubIOS`) when finishing work.
- Don't commit IDE cruft (`*.xcuserstate`, `.DS_Store`).
